changes.finding: CONFIRMED. The authoritative promoted signal intelligence repository is the knowledge_bus/packages/ directory tree. Each package directory may contain an optional promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml, opted-in via the 'promoted_signal_intelligence:' key in package_manifest.yaml. Schema authority: knowledge_bus/schema/promoted_signal_intelligence_schema_v1.yaml (status: LOCKED, version 1.0.0). Validator: backend/scripts/validate_promoted_signal_intelligence.py (exists). Loader: backend/core/knowledge/load_promoted_signal_intelligence.py (exists). Runtime status: NOT CONSUMED — ARCH-RT-5E deferred PSI from runtime (enforced by test). HARDENING NOTE 1: The PSI schema (LOCKED) does NOT contain lifecycle fields (runtime_activation_state, medical_review_status, scoring_readiness). These live in package_manifest.yaml (e.g. governance_runtime_activation_status: runtime_active_canonical). Cursor must not add these fields to promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml — the sprint report and Batch A manifest YAML are the correct artefacts for tracking lifecycle states per cluster. If the existing PSI schema cannot accommodate a required field, Cursor must stop and report, not extend the schema unilaterally. HARDENING NOTE 2: signal_system_allowed values are: metabolic, lipid_transport, hepatic, inflammatory, renal, vascular, hematologic, hormonal, mitochondrial, endocrine, bone, mineral, nutritional, other. 'cardiovascular' is NOT allowed. Cursor must map: cardiovascular lipid cluster → lipid_transport; kidney/eGFR → renal; thyroid → endocrine or hormonal; liver → hepatic; homocysteine/one-carbon → vascular. HARDENING NOTE 3: trigger_direction legacy value 'both' is obsolete — use bidirectional or context_dependent only.
